quotes.text: When I would stride out . . . : Here begins a remarkably vivid and extended picture of public life in an ancient town and the status of the town's great man.
quotes.speaker: Raymond P. Scheindlin
quotes.attr: Raymond P. Scheindlin, The Book of Job, W. W. Norton & Company New York, 1998, pp 205

notes: - Delivered the poor and fatherless; caused the widow's heart to sing for joy - Put on righteousness and justice like a robe and turban - Provided eyes to the blind and feet to the lame - Was a father to poor and searched out their case - Broke the fangs of the wicked and plucked the victim from his teeth
As he described the days gone by when he was respected by all, what things had he done? (29:12-17)

quotes.text: Nest. Figuratively, a nest is the dwelling place of a person or people (Job 29:18; Prov. 27:8; Jer. 48:28). Concerning Jesus, however, it is written that "the foxes have holes, and the birds of the air have nests; but the Son of man hath not where to lay his head" (Matt. 8:20). (See Dove; Eagle; Hen.)
quotes.attr: Joseph Fielding Mc Conkie and Donald W. Parry, A Guide to Scriptural Symbols, p.86

quotes.text: They would listen . . . waiting: as we too have been waiting for Job to pick up the colorful description of his audience in the town square, from which he had digressed, lost in satisfied recollection of his acts of charity. By this rhetorical maneuver, the author makes it seem as if the street scene had been briefly frozen and that it resumes its motion here.
quotes.speaker: Raymond P. Scheindlin
quotes.attr: Raymond P. Scheindlin, The Book of Job, W. W. Norton & Company New York, 1998, pp 205

quotes.text: When I smiled at them: When Job would smile at petitioners, they would fear his smile would not last and would take care to continue to please him.
quotes.speaker: Raymond P. Scheindlin
quotes.attr: Raymond P. Scheindlin, The Book of Job, W. W. Norton & Company New York, 1998, pp 205
